$comment: Console command-plane op catalog (P0, board round 1 applied). THIS FILE IS CANONICAL — the CONSOLE-CONTRACTS.md §5.2 table is generated from it; drift is a CI failure. The frontend renders ActionRows from this catalog; a UI button without an entry here is a defect (no op -> no button). Validated by op-catalog.schema.json. Field semantics: `lane` = coarse capability lane (closed enum: viewer|editor|operator|admin|term_admin) required on the Principal. `authz` = the per-resource check the op router MUST additionally pass: rule `own` (target resource must belong to the caller), `grant` (caller needs `relation` on one of `scope_any`, templates substitute op args), `own_or_grant` (own, else grant path), `read` (no resource authorization; scope filtering applies to the result). Both lane AND authz must pass BEFORE the intent audit. `human_only: true` = agents structurally excluded regardless of lanes. `executor` = the single liveness-gating executor (other parties are hops inside the effect). `testable`: disposable (sub-agents/temp DBs) | dry-run-only (only via dry_run:true outside prod) | live-canary (needs a written runbook). `confirm`: none | soft | typed-name (frontend renders; server enforces for typed-name via args.confirm_name). Args are JSON Schema fragments (draft 2020-12). Authz scope templates resolve in order: (1) op args by name (${handle}), (2) attributes of the loaded target resource (${target.project_id} — the router loads the resource named by the id arg before authorizing), (3) ${item.<field>} = nested arg path. An unresolvable entry within scope_any is SKIPPED (evaluation continues down the list); if ALL entries are unresolvable the op fails closed (scope_denied). CI rule: every ${var} must be a required arg, ${target.*} (declared resolution via the id arg), or ${item.*} nested-arg path. New authz rules: scope_visible (caller holds viewer on the TARGET's scope — for shared operational state like attention items), self (op only creates/affects caller-keyed state; no foreign-principal args accepted).

ops.effect: marks item claimed-by caller; dims + sorts below new; idempotent on id
auto-expiring snooze, visibly listed
channel-agnostic resolution; IDEMPOTENT on id (concurrent UI+agent resolve emits once); clears the one shared state
read: ready queue ordered by up_next/rank/priority; no audit emission (read op)
pin into the up-next lane

atomic CAS todo->doing + lease + fence++; two claimers never both win; loser gets error code claim_lost. Result carries the full lease INCLUDING claim_token for AGENT callers only (delivered over the authenticated channel, never persisted client-side by browsers); browser callers get leasePublic + a server-held session lease. SECRETS: the dedup-recorded result is stored SCRUBBED (idempotent replay returns leasePublic; a lost token is re-driven via CAS -> claim_lost); the task.claimed emission carries leasePublic ONLY; task.claim results are on the self-instrumentation never-capture list

lease.description: REQUIRED for agent callers on lease-guarded transitions (out of doing: review|blocked|done). Tracker rejects stale fence (stale_fence) and wrong holder (not_lease_holder). NEVER supplied by browsers.

force.description: Human principals with server-resolved commit authority for this target may force a transition without holding the lease; permission-level names are data and are never checked here; audited; tracker clears the lease + increments fence.

ops.effect: status/priority/blockers/verification transitions; store guards illegal transitions (illegal_transition); verify = verification_status:verified + status:done in ONE call (the store cascades); reject = verification_status:rejected + status:todo + lease cleared
close with reason (why-ledger); same lease/force rules as task.update

files/loads tracker task, posts task-card; sender_class stamped from the Principal (never honored from args); interrupt policy enforced dispatcher-side (spoofed principal_command demotes to defer); absent recipient = pool card, authorized via the fleet entry (the agent:${recipient} entry is skipped when unresolvable)
start the supervised session; ASYNC: result status=accepted, completion via agent.lifecycle emission carrying the op id
stop the supervised session; ASYNC as agent.start
restart; allowed on stale target state (recovery gates on manager liveness; confirm notes staleness); ASYNC as agent.start

confirm_name.description: must equal handle (compared post-normalization per Rule 4)

ops.effect: mint a fresh session id — truly new conversation; destructive; ASYNC
set roster autonomy
manual governance override; edge-triggered like autonomous actions; ASYNC (accepted -> governance.action emission)
pin model tier (manager applies via model_override_path); ASYNC
fleet-wide mode broadcast; cascade auto-engages, this is the manual path; ASYNC

NEW: resolve channel_lock lockout. Semantics: the op targets the RIGHTFUL manager (the one holding heartbeat ownership for the handle); it re-asserts the single-owner lock and fences the contender out (contender's sends are already forbidden in lockout per N0.1; this op forces the contender's release + re-acquire). If the contender is on another host, its own manager receives a stand-down envelope. ASYNC; completion via channel.lock_changed

type_pattern.description: glob over emission type — the thing being muted (per-caller mute, not global)

ops.effect: timed per-user mute (Quiet 1h), auto-expires, visibly listed; storm auto-mute uses this attributed to system:bus

owner.description: defaults to caller; setting another owner requires the grant path

window.description: digest window: a daily HH:MM or an interval (30m, 4h); default 18:00

ops.effect: upsert standing subscription; interrupt tier only accepted where the pattern's severity floor can reach p0/safety/principal-command (else error interrupt_ineligible)
remove standing subscription
dead-letter triage: repost to the board (fresh fence)
park until capacity/wake
tool-surface alias of POST /api/v1/query; read as-user; returns query_ref; no audit emission (read op). SQL mode additionally requires lane operator
validate + register a panel spec (render is client-side; this pins provenance)
text panel with live proved inline stats
layout mutations as data on the 12-col grid model

branch.description: investigation lineage: parent_dashboard_id, parent_question, filters, selected_mark, assumptions

ops.effect: dashboard = Library item (kind artifact; Phase 1 ships the minimal items table this rides on)
read; shared dashboards re-run queries AS the viewer (scope refusal is per-panel); no audit emission (read op)
pin as the per-user home (is_home flips in the /dashboards projection)
add a panel to the caller's home dashboard
delete a saved dashboard (own dashboards; soft confirm — dashboards are cheap, unlike library item.delete)
grant view/edit on a dashboard within the sharer's scope (a scope-grant on the item; viewers' panels still re-run as-viewer)
clicked context -> most recent chat message of the caller's assistant session (SelectedMark-complete: field+value+datum)
hybrid BM25+dense+RRF, scope-filtered below the ranker, why-matched returned; no audit emission (read op)
sole-egress research: check-before-web, write-back via consolidation gate; rate-limited per caller (query bodies leave the perimeter — logged) (egress gated: lane editor+, not plain viewer)
create item in a scope the caller can edit
update; prose merges CRDT; status via MV-Register (CONFLICT surfaces, never silent LWW)
grow-only typed link; requires viewer on to_id (a foreign supersedes/duplicate-of cannot distort a target you cannot see)
held-shelf: hold an item aside for a user
reviewable curation proposal with blast radius
apply proposal (human-gated: requires owner relation, held by humans)
reject with note
invalidate (reversible)
forward supersede, idempotent
rare, human-gated (owner relation, human principals); never offered to agents
personal-draft -> verified-shared request
approve promotion
restart a container/service on the host; ASYNC (accepted -> service.lifecycle)

confirm_name.description: must equal service (compared post-normalization per Rule 4)

ops.effect: stop a service; destructive; ASYNC

BOUNDED log snapshot in the result payload (P0). Live streaming is carved out with the term.* frame contract (§11); no audit emission (read op); the Rule-6 secret scrubber/redaction applies to the returned log snapshot

target.description: MUST be a registered probe target (service in the probe registry) — arbitrary URLs/IPs rejected (ssrf_rejected); no user-supplied network destinations

ops.effect: re-check availability now

confirm_name.description: must equal box_id (compared post-normalization per Rule 4)

ops.effect: reboot the box; destructive; ASYNC
collector re-check now; ASYNC
staged-approval: record approval (console-api is the approval ledger); apply is separate; undo = revoke approval before apply
apply approved updates; availability = apply_mode x agent_vs_agentless x executor liveness (agentless boxes: op never exists -> caption, not disabled); ASYNC
Key Ceremony approve: TokenAuthority mints the enrollment binding (gating executor = control-plane; the edge is notified as a hop); closes the enrollment gap
deny pending enrollment

confirm_name.description: must equal the enrolled handle (compared post-normalization per Rule 4)

ops.effect: revoke enrolled key; sessions drop; destructive
drop a live session; ASYNC
ask the agent's manager to redial; rides the Matrix floor when the tunnel is down; ASYNC
open read stream (concurrent streams capped); audit line commits BEFORE first frame or the stream never opens; stream torn down immediately on lane/grant revocation
escalate to write mode (entry-by-consent); stream ops are valid ONLY for the principal that opened the stream (cross-principal use = a new audited term.watch)

keystrokes; input stored as sealed blobs (opening is itself audited, 90d retention); the request envelope is EXCLUDED from access logs and console.api.* self-emissions; stream ops are valid ONLY for the principal that opened the stream (cross-principal use = a new audited term.watch)
resize; stream ops are valid ONLY for the principal that opened the stream (cross-principal use = a new audited term.watch)
fetch scrollback; no audit emission (read op within an audited stream); stream ops are valid ONLY for the principal that opened the stream (cross-principal use = a new audited term.watch)
close stream; stream ops are valid ONLY for the principal that opened the stream (cross-principal use = a new audited term.watch)
send vetted test body via Matrix; rate-capped (3/hour); result renders the receipt honestly

target.description: MUST be an address provably bound to the owner: the owner's own Matrix id, or a room the owner's verified Matrix id is joined to (checked at set time + on delivery). Arbitrary rooms rejected (target_not_owned) — interrupt-tier signals carry user-scoped data

ops.effect: set + auto-test; verified flips only on a delivered receipt; receipts scoped user:<owner>

receipt_ref.description: seq of the failed delivery.receipt emission

ops.effect: re-deliver a failed delivery (only where the receipt marks retryable); an op with an audit line like any other; the receipt at receipt_ref MUST be scoped user:<caller> (foreign receipt refs -> scope_denied; seq guessing yields nothing)
quiet hours; echoed as cocoon_until in the /delivery read; severity p0 + interrupt_policy safety ALWAYS pass (enforced at dispatch)
